query: What are the leading authorities for predatory pricing? Give me a short memo.
expected_case_names: Brooke Group Ltd. v. Brown & Williamson Tobacco Corporation
Weyerhaeuser Co. v. Ross-Simmons Hardwood Lumber Co., Inc.
key_holding_phrases: price below an appropriate measure of cost
notes: Brooke Group is the foundational two-part test (below-cost + recoupment). Weyerhaeuser extends Brooke Group to predatory bidding on the buy side.

query: What must a plaintiff prove to establish monopolization under Section 2 of the Sherman Act?
expected_case_names: Aspen Skiing Company v. Aspen Highlands Skiing Corporation
Verizon Communications, Inc. v. Law Offices of Curtis V. Trinco, LLP
notes: Aspen Skiing on refusal to deal; Trinko on limits of refusal-to-deal liability; Spectrum Sports on attempt-to-monopolize (dangerous probability test).

query: When is resale price maintenance (RPM) per se illegal? What is the current standard?
expected_case_names: Leegin Creative Leather Products, Inc. v. PSKS, Inc.
notes: Leegin overruled Dr. Miles Medical Co. v. John D. Park & Sons Co. (1911) and held that vertical minimum RPM agreements are judged under the rule of reason, not per se illegal.

query: What is the state action doctrine in antitrust and when does it immunize private conduct?
expected_case_names: North Carolina Board of Dental Examiners v. Federal Trade Commission
FTC v. Phoebe Putney Health System
notes: NC Dental: active supervision required when regulatory board controlled by active market participants. Phoebe Putney: clear articulation prong requires foreseeability of anticompetitive effect.

query: How has the Supreme Court analyzed antitrust claims in two-sided markets?
notes: Ohio v. Amex: two-sided transaction platforms must be evaluated as a whole; anti-steering provisions not anticompetitive when procompetitive effects outweigh harm. Apple v. Pepper: direct purchaser standing (Illinois Brick) applies to app store buyers.

query: What pleading standard applies to antitrust conspiracy claims under Section 1?
notes: Twombly replaced Conley's notice-pleading standard with plausibility pleading for antitrust conspiracy claims; parallel conduct alone insufficient without plus factors.

query: Can an NCAA rule restricting athlete compensation violate antitrust law?
expected_case_names: National Collegiate Athletic Association v. Alston
notes: Unanimous Alston decision: NCAA rules limiting education-related compensation violate Sherman Act § 1 under rule of reason; Court declined to apply a special standard for amateur sports.
